section.heading: Penalties for neglect or refusal to answer subpena

content.p: Any person who has been subpenaed under the provisions of subsection (d) of section 336 of the Immigration and Nationality Act to appear at the final hearing of an application for naturalization, and who shall neglect or refuse to so appear and to testify, if in the power of such person to do so, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than five years, or both.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Subsection (d) of section 336 of the Immigration and Nationality Act, referred to in text, is classified to section 1447(d) of Title 8, Aliens and Nationality.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1994—Pub. L. 103–322 substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1990 Amendment; Savings Provisions
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 101–649 effective Nov. 29, 1990, with general savings provisions, see section 408(a)(3) and (d) of Pub. L. 101–649, set out as a note under section 1421 of Title 8, Aliens and Nationality.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1981 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 97–116 effective Dec. 29, 1981, see section 21(a) of Pub. L. 97–116, set out as a note under section 1101 of Title 8, Aliens and Nationality.
